user.name: DAIR.AI
user.description: Democratizing AI research, education, and technologies. Learn about AI Agents for FREE at https://t.co/HHXg8rryu4

fullText:
New research with Microsoft's and colleagues on training agents inside the harnesses they actually run in.

(bookmark it)

Why it matters:

Agents today live inside elaborate harnesses like Claude Code, Codex, and OpenClaw.

Those harnesses are hard to train end to end because open RL stacks cannot express stateful, multi-process harness inference. Most training happens in stripped-down environments that look nothing like deployment.

OpenForgeRL closes that gap. A lightweight proxy serves the harness model calls while recording them as training data for a standard RL codebase, and a Kubernetes orchestrator runs each rollout in its own container. You train directly in the real harness, at scale.

Using only hundreds to a few thousand tasks, OpenForgeGUI reaches 72.3 on WebVoyager, 63.0 on Online-Mind2Web, and 37.7 on OSWorld-Verified, beating open baselines of similar size and matching models several times larger.

Harness choice turns out to be a training variable. Some harnesses are much harder to learn than others, and RL improves self-verification and multi-step completion while error recovery stays weak. That is a useful map for anyone doing agentic RL.

Paper: https://t.co/7CJEoHpJuo

Learn to build effective AI agents in our academy: https://t.co/LRnpZN7L4c

user.name: elvis
user.description: Founder @dair_ai • Prev: Meta AI | PhD • Learn about AI Agents for FREE here: https://t.co/P5SA9u54xO

fullText:
// Agentic Context Management //

Great read for the weekend.

(bookmark it)

Production agents fail less on reasoning and more on what sits in their context. Conversation history, big prompts, huge tool definitions, and ballooning tool outputs pile up every turn.

The common response is storage and retrieval, a place to stash memories and look them up. New research argues that framing is too narrow and names a fuller discipline, Agentic Context Management.

It decomposes into five primitives, architecting, ingesting, scoping, anticipating, and compacting with consolidation.

Naive accumulation grows token cost with the square of conversation length. Crude summarization buys linear cost but hits an accuracy cliff. Only compaction validated against fidelity gets you linear cost without losing what matters.

A reference implementation reports 92% on LongMemEval and 93.2% on LoCoMo.

Why does it matter?

Context management is becoming a first-class production concern that operates across an entire organization. The five primitives give you a structured way to reason about where your token budget goes.

Paper: https://t.co/mKdZNVnqLv

Learn to build effective AI agents in our academy: https://t.co/1e8RZKs4uX

fullText:
New release — "Matrix Multiplication Workbook: Fill-in-the-Blank Puzzles to Build Real Intuition by Hand" — at https://t.co/XTB3vByJMq by @ProfTomYeh 

𝙆𝙚𝙮 𝙁𝙚𝙖𝙩𝙪𝙧𝙚𝙨:
●You work by hand, with real numbers
●You fill in the blanks instead of reading proofs
●You reason about shapes, rows, columns, and structure step by step
●You discover the math yourself, rather than being told the rules upfront

𝙒𝙝𝙖𝙩 𝙔𝙤𝙪 𝙒𝙞𝙡𝙡 𝙇𝙚𝙖𝙧𝙣:
●Multiply matrices confidently using correct shapes and rules
●Recognize identity matrices, scaling, shifting, and their effects
●Understand why matrix multiplication is not commutative
●Apply associativity and distributive properties to simplify work
●Use chaining and transpose concepts in real computations
●Build intuition for inverse matrices and linear equations
●Strengthen speed and accuracy through progressive practice sets
●Learn tiled multiplication ideas for efficient computation

user.name: NVIDIA AI
user.description: Teaching your AI new tricks.

fullText:
We cut DeepSeek-V4 Pro startup from 8 minutes to under 2 minutes by moving weights over the fastest path to GPU memory with GPU-to-GPU RDMA.

This was achieved using NVIDIA ModelExpress (MX), the weight distribution and cache management service in NVIDIA Dynamo, and this same approach speeds up both inference and RL post-training too. MX reuses kernel caches, while inference workers fetch updated weights directly from other GPUs over NIXL—avoiding centralized broadcasts and keeping weight movement off the critical path.

user.name: Gary Marcus
user.description: OG GenAI Skeptic; spoke at US Senate. Warned about hallucinations in 2001. Advocating world models & neurosymbolic AI ever since. Author, Marcus on AI & 6 books

fullText:
What people are missing about AGI is that it’s not (just) the 5 things quoted out of context that I happened to mention in a 2023 open letter to Elon Musk, it’s about being *generally* intelligent, and able to do *all* the things that expert humans can do. 

There’s no sign that we have actually reached that point.

P.S. I stand by my prediction that it might happen next decade but that we won’t reach it this decade.

fullText:
The real comparison to Moore's law for AI isn't scaling laws, but rather the intelligence efficiency that we gain year-over-year using models to get better at training models. 

If we gain 2x per year on efficiency of training (ie lowering inference cost at fixed performance), in 5 years a given performance level will be 32x cheaper and in 10 1000x cheaper. 

That's when intelligence becomes a commodity like electricity. This will be a wonderful world to live in, regardless of if scaling the biggest models keeps yielding improvements.

fullText:
Have we reached AGI? Of course not.

Here is a reminder of my December 30, 2024 bet on with Miles Brundage that gives some *examples* of what we might expect (it’s not meant to be comprehensive).

I would expect a true AGI to be able to do all or nearly of these. 

I don’t think we are there or even close.  AFAIK nobody has systematically documented success on even one of them.

(Playing “gotcha” with a few examples from 2023 without any actual data doesn’t change that.)

fullText:
Recruiting online is about to change, and I won't ever want to read or send a résumé in my life!

I went through @meetdexai's interview process for testing purposes. It's a new AI talent agent for engineers, and I don't want to speak to a recruiter ever again.

If you are job hunting, you should give this a try.

The questions it asked me were the ones everyone should be asking:

• What problems do I want to spend my time on
• How big a team do I prefer to work with
• What a good week would look like for me
• ...

We all know résumés suck, and they are a poor representation of what you are capable of.

This is something very different:

Here, Dex builds a profile from a conversation with you. You can just speak to it (voice) or chat if you prefer. The AI will guide you through all the questions and build a profile it uses to show you jobs that match what you want.

You sign up with LinkedIn. You don't need to send a résumé. My interview took about 10 minutes.

From here, you get the list of roles that match, including salaries. You get a direct introduction to the hiring manager, with no other recruiter in the middle.

This is all free for candidates. Companies only pay when you get hired.

fullText:
openworker：吴恩达做的开源版cowork或workbuddy，一个住在你桌面上的开源 AI 同事。它交付的是结果，不是聊天——一份排好版的文档、一条带数字的 Slack 回复、一张理顺的日历、一个清过的收件箱。

官方原话："要一个结果，而不只是一个答案”

① 交付成品，不是给你一张待办清单
你说要的"结果"（"准备一份客户简报""把我这周日历理顺""看看这个 release 在 Jira 和 GitHub 上到哪步了"），它拆成步骤、跨桌面/文件/连接的 app 去做，最后给你能打开能分享的交付物。

② 本地优先 + 任意模型
agent 循环、对话、连接器 token、模型 key 全在你本机的密钥库里；唯一的云端部件只是个帮你走 OAuth 握手的小服务（你也能用手动凭证绕开、不登录也能用）。

③ 每个重要性动作都有审批
发消息、改日历、跑命令——任何要紧的操作都以"check-in"形式停下来等你点头；无人值守的定时任务不会自作主张，而是把"请求"停进一个收件箱等你处理。这跟"给它全权限让它自己跑"是两种安全观。

支持25+ 连接器
（GitHub/Slack/Jira/Notion/Linear/HubSpot/Gmail/日历…）+ 终端 + 本地文件，任何 MCP 工具也能插进来；能从 Slack 里 @ OpenWorker 触发、结果回到线程；能定时跑（晨报、周报、盯一个频道），带完整transcript；内置 Sales / 行政助理 / 市场 / Ops 值班几套角色模板。

一句话：AI 员工有很多云端产品，各种cowork、manus、workbuddy等，吴恩达这一步打的是本地员工。
#OpenWorker #AI员工 #吴恩达 #本地优先 #MCP #开源
 https://t.co/ntyOqUthdG

user.description: Advisor to startups. Freelancer. @LeadershipData founder. Global Speaker. Top B2B influencer & social promoter of Data Science AI ML. PhD Astrophysics @Caltech

fullText:
💥Scikit-learn Cookbook — 80+ recipes for Machine Learning in Python with scikit-learn [3rd Edition]: https://t.co/Z32D8jOzxC v/ @PacktDataML

𝓒𝓸𝓷𝓽𝓮𝓷𝓽𝓼:
🔹Common Conventions & API Elements of Scikit-Learn
🔹Pre-Model Workflow and Data Preprocessing
🔹Dimensionality Reduction Techniques
🔹Build Models with Distance Metrics & Nearest Neighbors
🔹Linear Models and Regularization
🔹Advanced Logistic Regression and Extensions
🔹Support Vector Machines and Kernel Methods
🔹Tree-Based Algorithms and Ensemble Methods
🔹Text Processing and Multiclass Classification
🔹Clustering Techniques
🔹Novelty and Outlier Detection
🔹Cross-Validation and Model Evaluation Techniques
🔹Deploying Scikit-Learn Models in Production

My Review (on Amazon):
I love this book, not only for its amazing content, but also for its brilliant adherence to its "Cookbook" label. The table of contents looks repetitive, but that's the magic of it. Find the algorithm, method, or application that you want "to cook up," and the presentation follows a uniform format from chapter to chapter. This is an extraordinarily practical, useful, and reader-friendly style, perfect for the target audience: anyone who needs to use (or is discovering how to use) specific Scikit-learn tools for their Python-based machine learning tasks. Consequently, this is great not only for individual use but also for training workshops and other educational settings. Python code in a cookbook context has never looked so good. IMHO.

fullText:
Codex 更新了，现在你可以在 Codex 里直接使用 GPT Live 实时的语音模型。

相较于手机端来说，这个体验简直太爽了！

你完全可以脱离键盘等任何输入设备，纯语音跟 Codex 交互。

比如你戴个带个 AirPods，就可以在家里的任何地方和它对话，用语音询问它生成的结果。

它还可以帮你执行 Codex 上的任何命令，这意味着你所有的 vibe coding 都可以脱离键盘和按键，只靠说话来完成了。

它会一直处于待命状态。如果你像官方演示那样，放个投影或者把电脑连接到电视上，就可以边干活、边玩游戏或做其他事。

即使是上厕所，你都能随时掌握你的 Codex 进度。我刚才简单试了一下，真的很爽！

fullText:
HOW HUAWEI IS USING PSEUDO 3D CIRCUITS TO BYPASS TRANSISTOR LIMITATIONS

Huawei is shifting chip design away from transistor size reduction and toward quasi 3D circuitry.

Here are 3 points about their new LogicFolding approach. 

1: This architectural shift leverages time based scaling to unlock higher compute density.

They talk a lot about their new scaling law. 

2: Managing thermal crowding is the challenge for such vertical chip design.

3: Semiconductor competition is evolving from heavy equipment purchasing to creative architecture and smart algorithmic routing.

Read here for more. 

#TechStrategy #SemiconductorInnovation #ChipDesign

https://t.co/Tgqr01zIaz

user.name: Jeffrey Towson 陶迅
user.description: I help increase online growth and strengthen digital AI moats. Digital & AI mgmt consultant. Get my free email and Tech Strategy podcast.

fullText:
CAN ROBOTS BE EMOTIONAL COMPANIONS?

At WAIC 2026, Fourier unveiled their GR Nano robot.

The Nano is a desktop-sized companion with emotional interactions.

It follows your voice and turns toward you when you speak.

It has micro-expressions and voice changes for emotional responses.

It reacts to touch (e.g., patting its head can trigger starry-eye effects or other cute reactions).

Price: $140

Fourier is an interesting robot biz. 

Founded in 2015, it began with exoskeletons and rehab devices used in hospitals and care centers.

Over time, Fourier had blended rehab-honed safety and precision with designs for close human care. 

The business has a compelling human-centric and care-focused design philosophy.

fullText:
Most company chatbots end with an answer.
GoalOS Chat Ω begins there.

Introducing the verified AI front door for every company—designed to understand customers, apply enterprise rules, retrieve approved knowledge, and turn conversation into completed work.

When an answer is enough, GoalOS answers—with evidence.

When action is required, it launches AGI Jobs:

→ define the mission
→ establish acceptance criteria
→ orchestrate agents, tools and systems
→ execute bounded work
→ verify the result
→ preserve the audit trail
→ deliver the outcome
→ learn only from what passed

Every interaction can become a real mission.

Every completed mission can create reusable capability.

Every verified capability can make the next interaction better.

One front door.
Every customer.
From conversation to verified action.

The decacorn-to-megacorn thesis:

Replace every company chatbot worldwide with GoalOS—the AI front door that doesn’t merely talk, but launches AGI Jobs and gets real work done.

#GoalOS #AGIJobs #EnterpriseAI #AgenticAI #MontrealAI

fullText: About 70% of America’s leading AI researchers are foreign‑born or foreign‑educated. The U.S. AI engine runs on global talent, it always has. Chinese AI talent, by contrast, is now returning in large numbers: recent analyses suggest roughly 70% of top Chinese AI researchers with overseas experience end up back in China, reversing an earlier pattern of near‑universal stay in the U.S.
